source.multipart_basis: RobotX WAM-V payload decks carry UAV launch pads, UUV deployment booms, and a sensor mast on a 4 x 2 m sandwich panel. Real competition decks are assemblies of a primary sandwich deck plate with bolted aluminum or CFRP truss / I-beam cross-members underneath, pontoon clamping brackets along each long edge, discrete payload hardpoints on top, and a mast foundation. Decomposition speculative at the level of cross-member count and payload hardpoint map; all load magnitudes, material, and pass/fail thresholds are preserved.

full_prompt:
Design a single-part monolithic payload deck for the 2026 RobotX WAM-V 16 USV, compliant with the 2026 RobotX Team Handbook. The WAM-V has two 5 m pontoons connected by a 4 m x 2 m deck bridging structure on top of which the team must mount sensors, a UAV launch/recovery interface, and a UUV deployment gantry. Payload mass limit 500 kg on the deck. The deck is a honeycomb sandwich composite panel: outer skin 3 mm carbon/epoxy quasi-isotropic, 25 mm Nomex honeycomb core, inner skin 3 mm carbon/epoxy. Overall dimensions 4000 mm x 2000 mm x 31 mm. Deck attaches to the WAM-V inflatable pontoon rigid inserts via 8 clamping strap interfaces on each long edge (16 total) - model as pinned. Material: carbon/epoxy (E1 = 135 GPa, E2 = 10 GPa, strength L = 1500 MPa, density 1600 kg/m^3); Nomex honeycomb (density 48 kg/m^3, out-of-plane compressive 2.4 MPa, shear 1.2 MPa). Skin layup [0/45/90/-45]s. Load cases: (R1) Distributed payload 500 kg x 9.81 = 4,905 N uniformly over deck center 2000 mm x 1000 mm area; max fiber stress <= 1500 / 3.0 = 500 MPa; max core shear <= 1.2 MPa; max deck deflection <= L/200 = 20 mm. (R2) Concentrated UAV launch load: 80 kg UAV + 3 g launch recoil = 2,354 N at a single 500 mm x 500 mm pad location near deck center; max fiber stress <= 500 MPa; max deflection at load <= 15 mm. (R3) UUV deployment: 25 kg UUV hoisted and swung, producing 50 kg equivalent dynamic load at the deployment boom rooted at deck edge => 490 N vertical + 200 N horizontal; max fiber stress <= 500 MPa. (R4) Wave-hogging asymmetric load: full 500 kg concentrated at one corner (worst case of waves lifting one pontoon), 4,905 N corner load; max fiber stress <= 500 MPa; max corner deflection <= 30 mm. (R5) Deck structural mass <= 60 kg. (R6) Deck first modal frequency pinned at 16 strap interfaces >= 15 Hz (decouple from wave excitation <= 1 Hz x 10 margin). Primary verification: laminate-composite structural FEA (linear static x4) + modal (R6). Vibration secondary via R6. Thermal, fluid, radiation NOT required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 deck panel, laminate schedule, strap-mount drawings, and a report listing deck mass in kg, max fiber stress per case, max deflection per case, first modal frequency in Hz.

--- ASSEMBLY RECONFIGURATION ---
The payload deck is delivered as a multi-part bolted assembly, not a single sandwich panel. Speculated families: (A) primary deck sandwich plate (4000 x 2000 x 31 mm CFRP/Nomex sandwich); (B) transverse I-beam / truss cross-members underneath the deck (x5, CFRP or Al extrusion, spaced ~800 mm along length) providing LC1/LC4 bending stiffness; (C) pontoon clamping strap brackets (x16, 8 per long edge, Al 6061 machined, bolted to A and clamping to pontoon inserts); (D) payload hardpoints on top of A (x12 mission hardpoints including one 500x500 UAV launch pad and one UUV boom base) for LC2/LC3; (E) mast foundation (reinforced bolted plate carrying the sensor mast); (F) edge rail / splash rail around A perimeter; (G) two longitudinal stringers under A long edges for additional bending stiffness; (H) UAV launch pad local reinforcement / sub-deck insert at the LC2 load patch. Interfaces: A<->B bolted through bonded-in inserts on A (cross-members provide stiffness under LC1/LC4); A<->C bolted at 16 strap positions (primary mounting BC); A<->D bolted hardpoints (bonded-in steel or Al inserts); A<->E bolted with local ply build-up; A<->F bolted (non-structural); A<->G bolted (longitudinal stringers mirror cross-members); A<->H bonded/bolted local insert. FEM verification runs on the assembled model ONLY. The LC1 distributed load is carried primarily by A + B in combined bending; LC2 UAV recoil concentrates at H and A; LC3 UUV swing loads go into D and A; LC4 corner hogging stresses the C bracket at the loaded corner. Inter-part DCR <= 1.0 under the governing case. All load magnitudes, material properties, skin layup, and pass/fail thresholds are preserved verbatim.
short_prompt:
Design the payload deck for the 2026 RobotX WAM-V 16 USV as a multi-part bolted assembly, compliant with the 2026 RobotX Team Handbook. The WAM-V has two 5 m pontoons connected by a 4 m x 2 m deck bridging structure; payload mass limit 500 kg.

Deck is a honeycomb sandwich composite panel: 3 mm carbon/epoxy outer skin, 25 mm Nomex honeycomb core, 3 mm carbon/epoxy inner skin; overall 4000 x 2000 x 31 mm; skin layup [0/45/90/-45]s. Attaches to pontoon rigid inserts via 8 clamping strap interfaces per long edge (16 total), pinned. Carbon/epoxy: E1 = 135 GPa, E2 = 10 GPa, longitudinal strength 1500 MPa, density 1600 kg/m^3. Nomex core: density 48 kg/m^3, compressive 2.4 MPa, shear 1.2 MPa.

LC1 distributed payload 500 kg x 9.81 = 4905 N over center 2000 x 1000 mm; max fiber stress <= 1500 / 3.0 = 500 MPa; core shear <= 1.2 MPa; deflection <= L/200 = 20 mm. LC2 UAV launch 80 kg + 3 g recoil = 2354 N on 500 x 500 mm pad; <= 500 MPa; deflection <= 15 mm. LC3 UUV deployment: 25 kg UUV swung (50 kg equivalent) => 490 N vertical + 200 N horizontal at deck-edge boom; <= 500 MPa. LC4 wave-hogging asymmetric 4905 N corner load; <= 500 MPa; corner deflection <= 30 mm. Deck structural mass <= 60 kg. First modal frequency pinned at 16 strap interfaces >= 15 Hz.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Fiber-stress per-ply is a laminate-theory post-processing step.

prompt.title: RobotX 2026 WAM-V payload deck (multi-part bolted assembly)
prompt.brief: Carbon/Nomex sandwich deck (4 x 2 m) with underdeck cross-members, pontoon clamping brackets, payload hardpoints, and mast foundation; carries 500 kg payload + UAV launch + UUV deployment loads. Multi-part: primary deck (A), cross-members (B x5), pontoon strap brackets (C x16), payload hardpoints (D x12), mast foundation (E), edge rail (F), longitudinal stringers (G x2), UAV pad reinforcement (H). FEM assembled-only; DCR <= 1.0.

material.cross_member_material: CFRP pultruded I-beam or Al 6061-T6 extrusion (80 x 40 x 5 mm I)

load_cases.name: UUV_deployment
load_cases.applied_to: D UUV boom base at deck edge

load_cases.name: modal
load_cases.description: pinned at 16 C strap positions; first frequency >= 15 Hz

boundary_conditions.mounting: 16 C strap brackets pinned to pontoon rigid inserts (8 per long edge)
boundary_conditions.loads: distributed and point loads on A surface and on D/H hardpoints
boundary_conditions.bolted_joints: A<->B cross-member bolts, A<->C strap bolts, A<->D payload hardpoint bolts modeled as stiffness-equivalent with VDI 2230 post-check

objective.design_heuristic: target assembled deck structural mass <= 60 kg

output_format.body_count: multi-body (A + B x5 + C x16 + D x12 + E + F + G x2 + H)

parts.name: primary_deck_sandwich
parts.role: main load-carrying deck plate; all payload reaches the pontoons through A

geometric_constraints.bolted_insert_pattern: cross-member bolts, strap-bracket bolts, hardpoint bolts (all via bonded-in inserts)

parts.name: underdeck_cross_member
parts.role: transverse I-beam / truss spaced along length, providing LC1/LC4 bending stiffness

geometric_constraints.cross_section_mm: 80 x 40 x 5 I-beam (Al) or equivalent CFRP
geometric_constraints.attachment: bolted to A via 8 bolts per cross-member through bonded inserts

parts.material_hint: CFRP pultruded I-beam or Al 6061-T6 extrusion

parts.name: pontoon_strap_bracket
parts.role: Al machined bracket bolted to A and clamping to the WAM-V pontoon rigid insert; primary mounting BC

geometric_constraints.clamp_interface: conforms to WAM-V pontoon insert (strap radius ~120 mm)

parts.name: payload_hardpoint
parts.role: mission-payload mounting point (sensors, deployment booms, winches) bolted to A at bonded-in inserts

geometric_constraints.insert_material: steel or Al 7075 bonded into A
geometric_constraints.distribution: 8 along central length, 4 at corners for camera/antenna mounts

parts.name: mast_foundation
parts.role: reinforced bolted plate carrying the sensor / radio mast and distributing its moment into A

parts.name: longitudinal_stringer
parts.role: 2 longitudinal stringers under A long edges adding bending stiffness along the deck length

geometric_constraints.attachment: bolted to A via ~10 bolts per stringer through bonded inserts; intersects with B at cross-member joints

parts.name: uav_launch_pad_reinforcement
parts.role: local ply build-up + bonded sub-deck insert at the 500x500 UAV launch patch; carries LC2 recoil

geometric_constraints.construction: CFRP local ply doublers (6 additional plies) + bonded-in Al 7075 block under the patch

assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: X along-deck, Y across-deck, Z up

pass_fail_criteria.description: Connection DCR across bolted interfaces (IF1..IF7) <= 1.0 under the governing load case.
Strap-bracket bolts (A<->C) shall not exceed bolt utilization 1.0 under LC4 corner hogging (worst C-bracket shear+tension).
pass_fail_criteria.metric: max bolt utilization at IF3 (strap brackets)

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: Generated coverage gate: ensures prompt-level envelope, body-count, report-field, and part geometry constraints are represented by an explicit geometry audit experiment.

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: sandwich laminated shell for A + beam/solid for B/G + solid for C/D/E/H inserts
connection_modeling: bolts as stiffness-equivalent MPC/CBUSH with VDI 2230 post-check
bonded inserts (D, H) as tied solid-to-shell
edge rail F as non-structural mass only

interfaces.mediated_by: bolted to bonded-in inserts in A; 8 bolts per cross-member (M8 Grade 8.8)
interfaces.load_path: primary LC1/LC4 bending reaction into cross-members

interfaces.mediated_by: bolted to bonded-in inserts along long edges; ~10 bolts per stringer
interfaces.load_path: adds longitudinal bending stiffness; shares LC1/LC4 load with B

interfaces.mediated_by: bolted to bonded-in inserts at 16 positions (4 M10 Grade 8.8 bolts per bracket)
interfaces.load_path: primary mounting BC; the corner bracket carries the worst LC4 hogging reaction
payload-point loads into deck; governs LC3 UUV boom base and concentrated mission loads

interfaces.mediated_by: bolted with ply build-up on inner skin
interfaces.load_path: mast moment into deck; wind and dynamic excitation

interfaces.mediated_by: bonded Al 7075 insert + ply doublers + 8 bolts clamping the UAV
interfaces.load_path: LC2 UAV recoil into deck; governs local fiber stress at the load patch
all deck loads reach the pontoons through these 16 straps

fem_check_policy.rationale: Deck bending under LC1/LC4 depends on cross-member spacing and longitudinal stringer stiffness; UAV recoil (LC2) depends on the local insert H; UUV boom (LC3) depends on hardpoint D. None of these can be evaluated with a single-body sandwich panel FEA.

notes.why_multi_part: Real WAM-V competition decks are bolted assemblies with underdeck trusses and discrete payload hardpoints; the single-body sandwich panel in the brief is a simplification.
notes.speculation_caveats: Cross-member count (5) and payload hardpoint count (12) are plausible defaults; actual team-specific layouts vary.

exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Wave excitation out of scope at deck level.

generated_eval_coverage.purpose: Add runnable CCX coverage kit for JSON-only multipart prompt.
generated_eval_coverage.note: Generated checks assert solver coverage for every FEA-grade requirement, every declared load case, and prompt-level geometry constraints; they are not a domain-specific certification model.

fea_experiments.metric: max bolt utilization at IF3 (strap brackets)
